questions.text: Name the orbital n = 5; l = 2; m = 0.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Quantum numbers are the basis of the sequence, arrangement, shape, and orientation of the elements in periodic table. N = 5 indicates the 5th energy level (5th period of the periodic table or the 5th principal quantum number) L = 2 indicates the shape of the orbital (2 corresponds d-orbital-shaped quantum number) M = 0 indicates the orientation of the orbital (z - axis magnetic quantum number)

questions.text: Which of the following shows the Planck's quantum theory?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: In chemistry, Frequency is denoted by vSo, the Planck's quantum theory isE = hv where h is the Planck's constantIt can be rewritten asE = hc/λ since frequency(v) = c/λ where c is the speed and λ is the wavelength.

questions.text: The shielding effect of inner electrons is responsible for:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Having no effect on ionization energy

questions.explanation: The shielding effect of inner electrons causes a decrease in attraction between the nucleus and the outer electrons of the atom. Hence, it is easier to remove the outer electrons, which decreases the ionization potential. Therefore, options B and C are incorrect Option D is incorrect because 'Electronegativity' is a measure of an atom's ability to attract shared electrons to itself, and due to the decrease in attraction between the nucleus and the outer electrons, electronegativity will also decrease.

questions.text: Maximum number of electrons in a subshell is given by:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: l = azimuthal quantum numberl values: s=0 , p=1, d=2, f=3Number of orbitals in a subshell: 2l+1You can check this for the 1st shell: 2(0) +1= 1Number of electrons in a shell = 2 x number of orbitals (as each orbital has a maximum of 2 electrons)Therefore,2(2l+1)

questions.text: Orbitals having the same energy are called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: In chemistry, orbital hybridisation is the concept of mixing atomic orbitals to form new hybrid orbitals suitable for the pairing of electrons to form chemical bonds in valence bond theory.

Electron orbitals having the same energy levels are called degenerate orbitals. Degenerate orbitals are the orbitals of the same subshell having equal energies. When the electrons of the orbitals are not influenced by any external factors like an electric field or magnetic field, they have the same energies.

The valence shell is the set of orbitals which are energetically accessible for accepting electrons to form chemical bonds. For main-group elements, the valence shell consists of the ns and np orbitals in the outermost electron shell.
Sub orbitals are the orbitals with the S P D and F shells that have their own unique quantum numbers and spin patterns when electrons are treated as waves.

questions.explanation: Degenerate orbitals are the orbitals of the same subshell having equal energies. When the electrons of the orbitals are not influenced by any external factors like an electric field or magnetic field, they have the same energies.

questions.text: Quantum number values for 2p orbitals are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: There are two types of quantum numbers: azimuthal quantum number(l) and principal quantum number(n). The principal quantum number,n, is actually the number of shells. Azimuthal quantum number, l, is actually the number of subshells. According to n-1 rule for the azimuthal quantum number, l will be equal to 1. This option represents 2p orbital. 2 represents the “n=2” and p represents the second subshell hence “l=1”.
It represents 2d orbital.
It represents 2s orbital.
It represents 1s orbital.

questions.explanation: There are two types of quantum numbers: azimuthal quantum number(l) and principal quantum number(n). The principal quantum number,n, is actually the number of shells. Azimuthal quantum number, l, is actually the number of subshells. According to n-1 rule for the azimuthal quantum number, l will be equal to 1. “2p”, where 2 represents the “n=2” and p represents the second subshell hence “l=1”.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is correct?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Faraday's experiment proved the existence of electrons
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. Faraday's experiments, particularly his work on electromagnetism and electrochemistry, did not directly prove the existence of electrons. His experiments focused on the relationship between electricity and magnetism, as well as the phenomenon of electrolysis. The discovery of electrons came later through the work of J.J. Thomson and his cathode ray experiments.

options.text: Crookes tube experiment shows the presence of electrons and protons in the atoms
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. The Crookes tube experiment, conducted by Sir William Crookes, aimed to study the properties of cathode rays. Although the experiment provided evidence for the existence of negatively charged particles (later identified as electrons), it did not demonstrate the presence of protons in atoms. The discovery of protons as constituents of the atomic nucleus came later through the experiments conducted by Ernest Rutherford.

options.text: Radioactivity confirms the presence of electrons and protons
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. Radioactivity, the spontaneous emission of particles or radiation from certain elements, does not directly confirm the presence of electrons and protons. Radioactivity is primarily associated with the decay or transmutation of atomic nuclei, involving processes such as alpha decay, beta decay, or gamma radiation. These phenomena are related to the instability of atomic nuclei and the release of specific particles or energy, but they do not directly provide evidence for the presence of electrons and protons.

options.text: Chadwick experiment shows the presence of neutrons
options.explanation: Chadwick carried out his own experiments and confirmed that he had finally found the neutron. Chadwick bombarded a sample of beryllium with alpha particles and observed a new form of radiation that was not affected by electric fields. He concluded that this radiation consisted of neutral particles, which he named neutrons. Neutrons have no electric charge but have a mass similar to protons.

questions.explanation: Option D is correct. Chadwick bombarded a sample of beryllium with alpha particles and observed a new form of radiation that was not affected by electric fields. He concluded that this radiation consisted of neutral particles, which he named neutrons. Neutrons have no electric charge but have a mass similar to protons.

questions.text: "In an atom no two electrons can have the same set of four Quantum numbers" is stated by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Heisenberg gave the uncertainty principle states that we cannot know both the position and speed of a particle simutaneously, such as a photon or electron, with perfect accuracy; the more we nail down the particle's position, the less we know about its speed and vice versa.
The Aufbau principle states that electrons fill lower-energy atomic orbitals before filling higher-energy ones.

Pauli's exclusion principle states that in an atom no two electrons can have the same set of four quantum numbers. Let us take example of electrons in Helium, both have all quantum numbers same except spin quantum number, one is clockwise and other is anti-clockwise.
Hund's rule: every orbital in a subshell is singly occupied with one electron before any one orbital is doubly occupied, and all electrons in singly occupied orbitals have the same spin.

The order in which the energy of orbitals increases can be determined with the help of the (n+I) rule, where the sum of the principal and azimuthal quantum numbers determines the energy level of the orbital. Lower (n+l) values correspond to lower orbital energies.

questions.explanation: The set of numbers used to describe the position and energy of the electron in an atom are called quantum numbers. There are four quantum numbers namely, principal, azimuthal, magnetic and spin quantum numbers. Pauli's exclusion principle states that in an atom no two electrons can have the same set of four quantum numbers. Let us take example of electrons in Helium, both have all quantum numbers same except spin quantum number, one is clockwise and other is anti-clockwise.

questions.text: The radii of the second orbit of the hydrogen atom calculated from Bohr's model is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This value, 0.529, is actually a constant used in Bohr's model to relate the radius of an electron orbit to the principal quantum number (n). It is not the specific value for the radius of the second orbit. The correct calculation for the second orbit using Bohr's model gives a value of approximately 2.116 angstroms, not 0.529.

4.8 is not the correct radius for the second orbit of the hydrogen atom according to Bohr's model. The radius calculated using Bohr's model for the second orbit is approximately 2.116 angstroms, not 4.8.

Let us consider the formula for the nth Bohr orbit, r{n} = (n²h²)/(4π² mZ e²). For a hydrogen atom, the atomic number z=1, first orbit n=1 r{1} = (h²)/(4πr² m e²) = 0.592A° This is the radius for the first Bohr orbit of the hydrogen atom. So the radius for the second Bohr orbit will be: 0.529 x n²/Z =0.529 x 2²/1= 2.116 A° so option C is correct.
3.4 is not the correct radius for the second orbit according to Bohr's model. The accurate value is approximately 2.116 angstroms, not 3.4.
The value 1 is too small to be the radius of the second orbit in Bohr's model. The radius calculated for the second orbit is approximately 2.116 angstroms, not 1.

questions.text: The ground state of an atom corresponds to a state of :
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ground state is the state of lowest energy and most stability hence, option A is incorrect.
This option is correct because the ground state of an atom is an area which is most stable that means it has least energy, thus ground state is the lowest energy level.
Minimum energy is the most appropriate option.
Minimum energy is the most appropriate option.

questions.explanation: The nucleus of an atom is surrounded by electrons that occupy shells or orbitals of varying energy levels. The ground state of an electron, the energy level it normally occupies, is the state of lowest energy for that electron.

questions.text: Shape of the orbital occupied by an electron is defined by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The principal quantum number determines the main energy level or shell in which an electron resides.
Option B is correct. Different quantum numbers determine different aspects of an orbital.The quantum number that determines the shape of an orbital is azimuthal quantum number.
Spin quantum number describes the intrinsic angular momentum of a particle.
Magnetic quantum number indicates the orientation of an orbital within a subshell.

questions.explanation: The shape of the orbital can be s,p,d, or f. This shape is determined by the azimuthal quantum number. The spin quantum number tells us about the type of spin electron possessed in an orbital. The principal quantum number tells us about the no.of the shell, not the shape of the orbital.

questions.text: The Balmer series lies in which region of the electromagnetic spectrum?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Lyman series of hydrogen atoms lie in the Ultraviolet region.
The Balmer series lies in the visible region of the electromagnetic spectrum.
Paschen series lies in the near Infrared region.
Bracket, Pfund as well as Humphrey series lie in the far Infrared region of the electromagnetic spectrum.

questions.explanation: The Balmer series lies in the visible region of the electromagnetic spectrum.

questions.text: If uncertainty in the position of an electron is zero, the uncertainty in it's momentum is?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: As per the explanation,this option is incorrect.
As per the explanation,this option is incorrect.
As per the explanation,this option is incorrect.
As per the explanation,this option is incorrect.

According to Heisenberg's uncertainty principle, it is impossible to measure position x and momentum p simultaneously. Use ∆x∆p ≥ h/4π as ∆x → 0, ∆p → ∞ Here, neither uncertainty can be zero and if the position is zero the uncertainty in momentum becomes infinite.

questions.explanation: According to Heisenberg's uncertainty principle, it is impossible to measure position x and momentum p simultaneously. Use ∆x∆p ≥ h/4π as ∆x → 0, ∆p → ∞ Here, neither uncertainty can be zero and if the position is zero the uncertainty in momentum becomes infinite.

questions.text: Diamond is a bad conductor because it:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Diamond has a tight structure as the carbons are held together by strong covalent bonds which makes it very hard making it useful for cutting, but it does not affect the conduction.
Diamond is very dense due to closely packed carbon atoms, however it doesn't effect the conduction of diamonds.

options.text: Has no free electron in the crystal
options.explanation: Each carbon atom present is covalently bonded to four other carbon atoms leaving no free electrons and thus does not conduct electricity
The bonds in diamond are held in such a tight structure that allows the light to pass around making it look transparent.

questions.explanation: A good conductor has a large presence of free electrons, as they are not bonded strongly to the nucleus, and allow conduction of charges to take place.
